user_query:
I'm coordinating our history club's weekly sync and want to share my extra reading in a clean, team-friendly way. Please: 1) Extract structured historical facts from all of these sources: - input/notes/medieval.html - input/notes/ancient.html - input/notes/revolutions.html - input/notes/book_notes.txt Produce a UTF-8 CSV at output/facts.csv with this exact header and column order: source_file,topic,year,location,person,tag,summary Rules: - source_file: the relative path to the input file for that fact (e.g., input/notes/medieval.html). - topic: required. - year: parse as an integer when present (BCE years are negative if shown as such); if no numeric year is given, leave blank. - location, person, tag: leave blank if missing. - summary: trim to 140 characters max. - Include every fact you can extract from each file based on the fields present in that file. - Sort the rows by year ascending (blank years last), then by topic ascending. Hints on where fields live in each file (useful for reliable extraction): - medieval.html: rows in the with columns Topic, Year, Location, Person, Tag, Summary. - ancient.html: each
in
has attributes data-topic, data-year, data-location, data-person, data-tag, and a

. - revolutions.html: each

  • in
      has (topic), , , , , and

      . - book_notes.txt: blocks separated by lines with --- use labeled lines Topic:, Year:, Location:, Person:, Tag:, Note: (Note -> summary). 2) Compose a concise club update at output/club_update.md with these sections: - Overview: list each unique source file and how many facts came from it in the format "filename: count" (use just the basename, e.g., medieval.html), and include a line "Total facts: X" that matches the row count of output/facts.csv. - Top Facts This Week: choose the five oldest facts by smallest numeric year (negative allowed); break ties by topic alphabetically. For each, include a bullet in the format "- Topic (Year): Summary" using the exact summary text from output/facts.csv for that topic/year. - Notes: 1–2 sentences telling the club that the full table is in output/facts.csv for planning (e.g., a quiz). 3) Rewrite input/drafts/message_draft.txt into a friendly, concise announcement for our group chat (under 120 words, enthusiastic but not over-the-top, no slang like "omg" or "lol"). Mention that the weekly update is saved at output/club_update.md and the detailed table is at output/facts.csv. Save the rewrite to output/message_rewrite.txt. Make sure all three outputs are created and internally consistent (counts in the markdown must match facts.csv). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
